relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Leviathan cannot be pulled up by Job’s hook.
No human rope or hook masters its face.
It does not plead softly for capture.
It will not make a servant-covenant with Job.
It cannot be made into a pet or plaything.
Merchants cannot reduce it to trade.
Harpoons and spears do not turn it into prey.
Touch it once and remember the battle.
Hope of mastering it collapses at its sight.
If none can rouse Leviathan safely, who can stand before the Maker?
Everything under heaven belongs first to the Divine.
Its body, strength, and frame are worthy of speech.
Its armor cannot be stripped away.
Its face opens into terror.
Its back is shield upon shield.
Its scales close so tightly no air passes between.
The armor holds as one.
Light flashes from it like dawn.
Fire imagery leaps from its mouth.
Smoke rises from its nostrils.
Its breath kindles flame.
Strength lodges in its neck, and dismay moves before it.
Its flesh is firm and unmoved.
Its heart is stone-like firmness.
Even mighty ones fear its rising.
Weapons do not stand against it.
Iron and bronze become weak before it.
Arrow and sling stone cannot drive it away.
It laughs at ordinary weapons.
Its underside tears the mud like a threshing sledge.
It makes the deep boil.
Its wake shines across the waters.
On earth, it is made without fear.
It stands as king over the proud wildness human power cannot tame.
